title: Aureline semantic-workspace-graph public edge-family boundary schema
description: Boundary schema for the public edge-family surface of the shared semantic-workspace-graph. This schema publishes one stable edge-family vocabulary every non-owning surface reads — search, docs, AI context assembly, topology maps, impact explorers, cited-explainer overlays, review packs, support bundles, and the public graph query family — so no consumer mints a private edge class, a private evidence-state axis, or a private durable-versus-derived edge classification. The canonical internal shapes (graph_edge_record, edge_class, edge_evidence_state, topology_edge_slot, provenance_stamp, freshness_frame, confidence_level, confidence_rollup, source_anchor, workset_scope_ref, impact_reason_slot, explainer_citation_slot) are owned by schemas/graph/workspace_graph_seed.schema.json; this file is the public contract surface that references those canonical types and groups every edge_class into a published edge family (containment, symbol_graph, module_graph, dependency_topology, ownership, documentation_and_citation, generated_artifact_lineage, imported_mirror, topology_runtime, impact, explanation, scoping, aliasing, missing_anchor) with publish-level rules for durable-versus-derived edge partitioning, evidence-state × durability interaction, the freshness-confidence compatibility rule for edges, and the published audit-event vocabulary for edge-family lifecycle. Raw source bodies, raw symbol definitions, raw document bodies, and raw provider URLs never cross this boundary. Adding a new published edge-family group, a new durable-evidence mapping, a new published-edge-constraint id, or a new published edge-family-audit-event id is additive-minor and bumps edge_family_schema_version. Repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

edge_family_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this edge-family export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries exactly one kind.

edge_family_group.description: Published edge-family groups. Every edge_class maps to exactly one group so consumers read 'which family does this edge belong to' without a local synonym. containment_family: contains (directory→file, file→symbol, file→generated_artifact). symbol_graph_family: defines_symbol, references_symbol. module_graph_family: imports_module. dependency_topology_family: depends_on. ownership_family: owned_by. documentation_and_citation_family: documented_by, cites. generated_artifact_lineage_family: generated_from. imported_mirror_family: mirrors_upstream. topology_runtime_family: deployed_to, runs_in, hosted_by, produces_artifact, consumes_artifact. impact_family: impacts. explanation_family: explains. scoping_family: scoped_by. aliasing_family: aliases. missing_anchor_family: missing_anchor_for.

durable_evidence_state.description: Public subset of edge_evidence_state that produces durable edge facts. Consumers MAY render these rows without a derived-state disclosure, provided the provenance source is in the durable_source_class set defined in schemas/graph/node_identity.schema.json.

derived_evidence_state.description: Public subset of edge_evidence_state that produces derived, stale, or honest-absence edge facts. Consumers MUST render a derived-state, staleness, or missing-anchor chip as appropriate; these rows MUST NOT upgrade to durable authority.

edge_durability_class.description: Published edge-durability class. durable_edge: evidence_state in durable_evidence_state, provenance source in durable_source_class. derived_edge: evidence_state = inferred_relation OR provenance source in derived_source_class; consumers render the derived chip. stale_edge: evidence_state = stale_relation; consumers render the staleness chip and the typed stale_reason. missing_anchor_edge: evidence_state = missing_anchor; consumers render the missing-anchor badge. Rule: a durable_edge MUST carry at least one source_anchor with an anchor_kind in the authoritative anchor set (filesystem_identity, symbol_definition_site, docs_pack_entry, mutation_journal_entry, generated_artifact_lineage, provider_resource_handle, imported_bundle_entry, replay_capture_entry, codeowners_rule).

freshness_confidence_compatibility_class.description: Published freshness-confidence compatibility class, shared with the node-identity boundary so edges and nodes render the same posture vocabulary. An edge's class MUST align with its freshness_frame, confidence_level, and edge_durability_class.

topology_edge_required_class_set.description: Frozen set of edge_class values that MUST carry a topology_edge_slot. Consumers that render topology maps, impact reports, or support exports read this list when checking whether a topology edge is missing its slot.

published_edge_constraint_id.description: Published edge-contract constraint ids. Consumers, test packets, and support bundles cite these verbatim. edge_id_stable_and_opaque: every record carries an opaque, stable edge_id; retired ids are never reused. edge_class_in_frozen_vocabulary: edge_class matches the frozen list in workspace_graph_seed.schema.json#/$defs/edge_class. endpoints_resolve_or_missing_anchor: from_node_id and to_node_id resolve to a published graph node or to a missing_anchor_node. evidence_state_in_frozen_vocabulary: evidence_state matches the frozen list. durable_edge_requires_authoritative_anchor: an edge with edge_durability_class = durable_edge carries at least one source_anchor in the authoritative anchor set. stale_edge_requires_typed_reason: an edge with evidence_state = stale_relation carries a typed stale_reason. missing_anchor_edge_requires_missing_anchor_endpoint: an edge with evidence_state = missing_anchor has at least one endpoint pointing at a missing_anchor_node. topology_edge_requires_topology_slot: an edge whose edge_class is in topology_edge_required_class_set carries a topology_edge_slot. explainer_citation_ref_reuses_graph_ids: explainer_citation_slot.citation_ref resolves to a graph node_id or edge_id (or a non-graph citation class). freshness_confidence_class_matches_frame: the published freshness_confidence_compatibility_class matches the record's freshness_frame + confidence_level + edge_durability_class triple. confidence_rollup_floor_respected: when confidence_rollup is present, the rolled_up_level is never strictly higher than the minimum of source_confidences; any unknown contributor pulls the rollup to at least low. no_raw_body_crosses_boundary: raw source bytes, raw provider URLs, and raw credentials never appear in any field of a published edge-family record.

edge_family_group.description: Published group the edge_class belongs to. MUST be the group the edge_class maps to in the edge_family_group vocabulary description; a mismatch is a contract violation.

topology_edge_slot.description: Required when edge_class is in topology_edge_required_class_set; forbidden otherwise.

published_notes.description: Redaction-aware reviewer notes carried on the published edge row. Never embeds raw source bodies, raw provider URLs, or raw credentials.

edge_family_record.description: One published edge-family record. Mirrors schemas/graph/workspace_graph_seed.schema.json#/$defs/graph_edge_record with one additional rule: every published row carries an edge_family_group, an edge_durability_class, and a freshness_confidence_compatibility_class so consumers render durable, derived, stale, cached, replayed, imported, warming, and missing-anchor edges with one vocabulary rather than a local synonym.

edge_family_audit_event_id.description: Frozen audit-event id vocabulary for the published edge-family surface. Support bundles, replay captures, and AI evidence packets cite these verbatim.

notes_ref.description: Opaque ref into structured notes. MUST NOT embed raw source bodies, raw provider URLs, or raw credentials.
